name: New Orleans Rubbed Chicken with Apricot Mustard Sauce
recipeIngredient: 4 small (4 ounces each) boneless, skinless chicken breasts, trimmed of visible fat
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine the dried thyme, ground black pepper, garlic powder, cayenne to taste (1/2 teaspoon), and salt.
2. Mix to blend the seasoning ingredients thoroughly.
3. Place the chicken on a large plate or platter.
4. Drizzle each chicken breast with olive oil until evenly coated.
5. Sprinkle the reserved seasoning mixture over the chicken.
6. Rub the seasonings into the chicken, ensuring it is evenly coated.
7. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 10 minutes to allow the flavors to penetrate the chicken.
8. Meanwhile, spoon the apricot preserves into a small microwaveable bowl.
9. Cook on low power in 15-second intervals until melted. Stir in the mustard until well blended.
10. Set aside the sauce.
11. Preheat the grill to high heat.
12. Place the chicken on the grill and reduce the heat to medium, moving away from direct heat.
13. Cook for 3 to 5 minutes per side until no longer pink and juices run clear.
14. Serve each breast with about 2 1/2 tablespoons of the reserved sauce spooned over the top.
Makes 4 servings
Per serving (1 chicken breast + 2 tablespoons of sauce): 197 calories, 26 g protein, 15 g carbohydrates, 3 g fat, <1 g saturated fat, trace polyunsaturated fat, 1 g monounsaturated fat, 66 mg cholesterol, 1 g fiber, 245 mg sodium.
